english: Not knowing is Buddha
meaning: Ignorance is bliss

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Even experts make mistakes

english: Even a packhorse driver looks good in fine clothes
meaning: Clothes make the person

english: Dumplings over flowers
meaning: Practicality over aesthetics; choosing substance over appearance

english: Time is money
meaning: Time is valuable and should not be wasted

english: If you're in a hurry, take a detour
meaning: The safest or most reliable way is often not the quickest

english: If you eat poison, eat the plate too
meaning: If you must do it, go all the way

english: Do laundry while the ogre is away
meaning: Take advantage of someone's absence

english: The nail that sticks out gets hammered down
meaning: Standing out invites criticism

english: Tap a stone bridge before crossing
meaning: Be very cautious

english: Just watching is a pleasure
meaning: Enjoy observing; you don't always need to participate

english: Fall seven times, get up eight
meaning: Never give up

romaji: Ishi no ue ni mo sannen
english: Three years on a stone
meaning: Perseverance leads to success; even a cold stone warms up if you sit on it long enough

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Even experts make mistakes

english: A bee on a crying face
meaning: Misfortune piles up

english: Failure is the origin of success
meaning: Mistakes lead to growth

japanese: 可愛い子には旅をさせよ
romaji: Kawaii ko ni wa tabi wo saseyo
english: Send the beloved child on a journey
meaning: If you love your children, let them face challenges and see the world rather than overprotecting them.

english: Counting the skins of tanuki you haven't caught
meaning: Don't count your chickens before they hatch; don't plan based on profits that aren't guaranteed yet.

english: Gold coins to a cat
meaning: Giving something of high value to someone who cannot appreciate or understand its worth.

english: Spilled water will not return to the tray
meaning: What's done is done and cannot be changed.

japanese: 二兎を追う者は一兎をも得ず
romaji: Ni to wo ou mono wa itto wo mo ezu
english: One who chases two rabbits catches neither
meaning: Focus on one thing at a time

japanese: 二度あることは三度ある
romaji: Nido aru koto wa sando aru
english: What happens twice will happen a third time
meaning: Patterns tend to repeat

english: The moon and a turtle
meaning: Vast difference; not comparable

english: Hurry to do good
meaning: If it's worth doing, do it at once

english: Continuity becomes strength
meaning: Consistent effort over time leads to success

japanese: 塵も積もれば山となる
romaji: Chiri mo tsumoreba yama to naru
english: Even dust, when piled up, becomes a mountain
meaning: Small efforts accumulate into great achievements

english: Continuity becomes strength
meaning: Consistent effort over time leads to success

english: Tap a stone bridge before crossing
meaning: Be very cautious

romaji: Tabi wa michizure yo wa nasake
english: Travel needs companions; life needs kindness
meaning: Relationships and compassion matter

english: Good medicine tastes bitter
meaning: Helpful advice often sounds harsh

japanese: 犬も歩けば棒に当たる
romaji: Inu mo arukeba bou ni ataru
english: Even a walking dog will hit a stick
meaning: Acting can lead to luck or trouble

english: A strong person under the veranda
meaning: An unsung hero who supports behind the scenes

japanese: 藪をつついて蛇を出す
romaji: Yabu wo tsutsuite hebi wo dasu
english: Poke the bush and bring out a snake
meaning: Stir up trouble unnecessarily

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well does not know the great sea
meaning: Limited experience limits perspective

english: If you eat poison, eat the plate too
meaning: If you must do something risky, go all the way

english: If in a hurry, go around
meaning: The safer path can be faster

english: Good medicine tastes bitter
meaning: Helpful advice can be harsh

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Good fortune comes to a laughing home
meaning: Happiness invites luck

japanese: 三人寄れば文殊の知恵
english: Three people together have the wisdom of Monju
meaning: Two heads are better than one

english: Not knowing is Buddha
meaning: Ignorance is bliss

english: A three-day monk
meaning: Someone who quits quickly

english: Even a packhorse driver looks good in fine clothes
meaning: Clothes make the person

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to a laughing gate
meaning: Happiness brings good fortune

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well does not know the great sea
meaning: Limited experience limits perspective

romaji: Ishi no ue ni mo san nen
english: Three years on a stone
meaning: Perseverance brings success

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well does not know the great sea
meaning: Limited experience limits perspective

english: The day you decide is a lucky day
meaning: Start now

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to a laughing gate
meaning: Happiness and good fortune come to those who smile

japanese: 三人寄れば文殊の知恵
english: Three people together have the wisdom of Monju
meaning: Two heads are better than one

english: Good causes bring good results
meaning: Good deeds lead to good outcomes

english: Sometimes losing is winning
meaning: Conceding can be the best outcome

english: Waking up early brings three coins of benefit
meaning: The early bird catches the worm

english: The mouth is the source of disaster
meaning: Careless words can cause trouble

romaji: Koubou ni mo fude no ayamari
english: Even Kobo makes mistakes with his brush
meaning: Even masters make mistakes

english: It is dark beneath the lighthouse
meaning: People often overlook what is right in front of them

english: The neighbor's lawn is green
meaning: The grass is always greener on the other side

romaji: Nou aru taka wa tsume wo kakusu
english: A capable hawk hides its claws
meaning: Truly skilled people do not show off

english: One time, one meeting
meaning: Treasure every encounter because it may never happen again

english: After rain, the ground hardens
meaning: Difficulties can make relationships or situations stronger

japanese: 案ずるより産むが易し
english: Giving birth is easier than worrying about it
meaning: Doing something is often easier than worrying about it

english: If you live there, it becomes the capital
meaning: Any place can become comfortable once you get used to it

japanese: 郷に入っては郷に従え
romaji: Gou ni itte wa gou ni shitagae
english: When entering a village, follow its customs
meaning: When in Rome, do as the Romans do

english: If the fish has a heart, the water has a heart too
meaning: Goodwill is often returned with goodwill

english: Acorns comparing their height
meaning: There is little difference between similar things

japanese: 飼い犬に手を噛まれる
english: Being bitten by the dog you raised
meaning: Being betrayed by someone you helped or trusted

japanese: 虎穴に入らずんば虎子を得ず
romaji: Koketsu ni irazunba koji wo ezu
english: Without entering the tiger's cave, you cannot get its cub
meaning: Nothing ventured, nothing gained

japanese: 人の振り見て我が振り直せ
romaji: Hito no furi mite waga furi naose
english: Look at others' behavior and correct your own
meaning: Learn from other people's mistakes

english: A bird leaving does not muddy the water behind
meaning: Leave things clean and proper when you depart

japanese: 聞くは一時の恥、聞かぬは一生の恥
romaji: Kiku wa ittoki no haji, kikanu wa isshou no haji
english: Asking is a moment of shame; not asking is a lifetime of shame
meaning: It is better to ask and learn than to remain ignorant

english: Do not forget your beginner's mind
meaning: Remember your original purpose and humility

english: Wait for good fortune while sleeping
meaning: After doing your best, wait patiently for the result

japanese: 好きこそ物の上手なれ
romaji: Suki koso mono no jouzu nare
english: Liking something makes you good at it
meaning: Passion helps people improve

romaji: Shoshinsha ni mo gobu no tamashii
english: Even a beginner has a measure of spirit
meaning: Everyone deserves respect, no matter their level

japanese: 明日は明日の風が吹く
romaji: Ashita wa ashita no kaze ga fuku
english: Tomorrow, tomorrow's wind will blow
meaning: Do not worry too much about tomorrow

japanese: 門前の小僧習わぬ経を読む
romaji: Monzen no kozou narawanu kyou wo yomu
english: A child near the temple gate recites sutras without being taught
meaning: People naturally learn from the environment around them

japanese: 腹八分目に医者いらず
english: Eating until eighty percent full keeps the doctor away
meaning: Moderation is good for health

japanese: 金の切れ目が縁の切れ目
romaji: Kane no kireme ga en no kireme
english: The end of money is the end of relationships
meaning: Some relationships depend on financial benefit

romaji: Hotoke no kao mo sando made
english: Even Buddha's face only lasts three times
meaning: Even a patient person has limits

japanese: 山椒は小粒でもぴりりと辛い
romaji: Sanshou wa kotsubu demo piriri to karai
english: A peppercorn is small but sharply spicy
meaning: Small things or people can still be powerful

japanese: 知らぬ間に時は過ぎる
romaji: Shiranu ma ni toki wa sugiru
english: Time passes before you know it
meaning: Time moves quickly, so use it wisely

english: One inch ahead is darkness
meaning: The future is uncertain

japanese: 喉元過ぎれば熱さを忘れる
english: Once it passes your throat, you forget the heat
meaning: People forget hardships once they are over

english: A frog's child is a frog
meaning: Children resemble their parents

english: Early rising is worth three coins
meaning: Getting up early brings benefits

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Experts make mistakes

english: Carelessness is the greatest enemy
meaning: Don't let your guard down

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to a laughing gate
meaning: Happiness and good fortune come to those who smile

english: An inch ahead is darkness
meaning: The future is uncertain

japanese: 三人寄れば文殊の知恵
english: Three people together have the wisdom of Monju
meaning: Two heads are better than one

japanese: 虎穴に入らずんば虎子を得ず
romaji: Koketsu ni irazunba koji wo ezu
english: If you don't enter the tiger's den, you can't get the cub
meaning: Nothing ventured, nothing gained

english: The mouth is the source of disaster
meaning: Careless speech can cause trouble

english: Fall seven times, get up eight
meaning: Never give up

english: A bee on a crying face
meaning: Misfortune piles up

japanese: 三人寄れば文殊の知恵
english: Three people together have the wisdom of Monju
meaning: Two heads are better than one

english: If you try, you can achieve it
meaning: Anything is possible if you believe in yourself and take action

english: Ten people, ten colors
meaning: To each their own; different people have different tastes

japanese: 虎穴に入らずんば虎子を得ず
romaji: Koketsu ni irazunba koji wo ezu
english: You can’t get the tiger’s cub without entering the lair
meaning: No reward without risk

english: It’s darkest under the lighthouse
meaning: The obvious is overlooked
